purpose: settings.parallel_computing=true on the exact parallel-computing-default program: Program.reason instantiates InterpretationParallel (program.py:42-43), whose entire diff from the serial optimized kernel is the decorator flip @numba.njit(cache=True, parallel=False -> True) on Interpretation.reason (interpretation_parallel.py:241) — the rules loop at :571 becomes a real prange. On this one-rule program the prange has width 1, so thread scheduling cannot reorder trace appends: every reasoning probe digest matched parallel-computing-default's at authoring (the kernels agree on this program) and only the knob readback differs — the nondefault-true-parallel class. Multi-rule prange scheduling is deliberately NOT exercised here (a wider program risks trace-order irreproducibility, which would be its own characterization case, not this pair's). Compile cost: the parallel kernel's numba compile took ~174s cold on this machine but CACHES across fresh processes (~3s warm) — the pinned docstring's claim that parallel disables caching (pyreason.py:204-205) is refuted on numba 0.59.1/darwin.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:parallel_computing nondefault-true-parallel; hello-world program with the knob set true
